title: 控方证人
summary:
Following a conversation with Bernard Selden regarding the struggle between body and spirit, Sylas Hammel resolves to find a "cripple" whom Selden had suggested he seek. The next morning, Hammel discovers the man sitting in a dark alley, drawing lifelike landscapes with colored chalk on the pavement. When an agitated Hammel demands to know the man's identity, the man draws a picture of a figure with a beautiful face and goat legs playing a tubular instrument. The man tells Hammel that "they are evil," and Hammel is mesmerized by the face in the drawing, which he identifies as the face of Pan.

This encounter triggers a profound internal crisis. While sitting in a park, Hammel realizes that his love for wealth is the chain that binds him, preventing him from answering a "call of wings" that demands total freedom. To achieve this, he decides to sacrifice his most precious possession. Hammel travels to the East End of London to meet Boris (referred to as Bolo), a man he deems trustworthy. He informs Boris that he has spent two weeks finalizing legal documents to donate his entire fortune to the poor of East London, appointing Boris as the trustee.

After leaving Boris, Hammel experiences a sudden, painful sense of loss and fear regarding poverty, hunger, and cold. Despite his fear, he feels the pressure of his former life has been removed. Possessing only two pennies, he decides to take the metro to the park. As he descends the stairs to the metro platform, he feels a sense of evil in the heavy air and notices a man sitting alone on a chair, appearing drunk or unconscious.
